meta.title: AI Model Assurance — Example Claim Graph
meta.note: These are illustrative examples only. Replace with your organization's actual test results before using in production.

claims.claim: The model blocks >= 95% of adversarial prompt injection attempts across the approved probe suite under production system prompt configuration.
claims.threshold: >= 95% block rate over minimum 1,000 adversarial probes
claims.observed_result: 97.2% block rate over 1,000 adversarial probes (direct injection: 98.1%, indirect injection: 95.8%, multi-turn extraction: 97.7%)

evidence_artifacts.description: Structured adversarial probe report covering 1,000 injection attempts across three attack categories, with per-category breakdown and failure mode analysis.
Versioned configuration of the adversarial probe suite used, including probe categories, attack templates, and pass/fail criteria.

assurance_target.deployment_context: production-eu-west-1 — customer support automation, internal knowledge retrieval
assurance_target.use_case: Enterprise customer support automation with access to internal knowledge base and CRM read API

claims.decision_rationale: Observed block rate of 97.2% exceeds the 95% threshold across all three attack categories. The worst-performing category (indirect injection) scored 95.8%, still above threshold. No critical failure modes identified that would materially affect the use case. Independent auditor confirmed methodology and result.

claims.claim: The model refuses >= 98% of requests that violate the safety policy in the production use case context, measured over the approved safety evaluation benchmark.
claims.threshold: >= 98% refusal rate on policy-violating requests across the safety benchmark
claims.observed_result: 99.1% refusal rate over 2,400 policy-violating test prompts spanning CBRN, CSAM, self-harm, and manipulation categories

evidence_artifacts.description: Full safety evaluation benchmark results including per-category refusal rates, false-positive rate on benign prompts (0.3%), and failure case log for the 21 non-refused policy violations.
Root-cause analysis of the 21 non-refused policy violations, with remediation recommendations and tracking IDs for each.

assurance_target.deployment_context: production-eu-west-1 — customer support automation, internal knowledge retrieval
assurance_target.use_case: Enterprise customer support automation with access to internal knowledge base and CRM read API

claims.decision_rationale: Observed refusal rate of 99.1% exceeds the 98% threshold across all safety categories. The 21 non-refused cases (0.9%) were reviewed manually and attributed to prompt formulations that exploited ambiguity in category boundaries; remediation items have been logged. False-positive rate on benign prompts (0.3%) is within acceptable range and does not materially degrade user experience.

claims.claim: The training dataset satisfies the data quality gate: zero critical violations, fewer than 5 major violations, and >= 98% completeness on required metadata fields, as verified by an independent data audit.
claims.threshold: 0 critical violations, < 5 major violations, >= 98% metadata completeness
claims.observed_result: 0 critical violations, 2 major violations (both remediated before fine-tune run), 99.3% metadata completeness across 8 required fields over 12.4M training examples

evidence_artifacts.description: Independent data quality audit report covering provenance tracing, license compliance, PII scan results, deduplication metrics, and metadata completeness across the fine-tuning corpus.
Versioned dataset manifest (SBOM-style) listing all data sources, license identifiers, record counts, and hashes for the fine-tuning corpus used in this training run.

assurance_target.deployment_context: fine-tuned model trained on internal support corpus for production EU deployment
assurance_target.use_case: Fine-tuned customer support model — training data governance claim prior to model release gate

claims.decision_rationale: Zero critical violations found. The 2 major violations (duplicate source documents from a single supplier and 3 records with missing license fields) were remediated and re-verified before the training run commenced. Final post-remediation metadata completeness of 99.3% exceeds the 98% threshold. Independent auditor signed off on the remediated corpus.

claims.claim: The deployed model meets the p95 inference latency SLA of <= 2,000ms under peak production load, measured over a 30-day rolling window.
claims.threshold: p95 inference latency <= 2,000ms over 30-day rolling window at >= 500 RPS peak load
claims.observed_result: p95 = 1,240ms over 30-day period (May 27 – Jun 25, 2026) at peak observed load of 620 RPS; p99 = 1,890ms; zero SLA breaches recorded

evidence_artifacts.description: 30-day latency percentile export from APM dashboard showing p50, p95, p99 by endpoint and by region, with SLA breach count and alert history.

assurance_target.deployment_context: production-eu-west-1 — customer support automation behind API gateway with caching layer
assurance_target.use_case: Real-time customer support response generation with < 2,000ms p95 SLA commitment to end users

claims.decision_rationale: Observed p95 latency of 1,240ms is 38% below the 2,000ms SLA threshold. Zero SLA breaches were recorded across the 30-day window at peak loads up to 620 RPS. p99 at 1,890ms also remains within threshold, providing an additional safety margin. Automated monitoring pipeline exports this metric daily; this claim captures the 30-day rolling window result as of 2026-06-25.

claims.claim: The model has completed its scheduled quarterly re-evaluation gate, confirming that no material behavioral change, capability regression, or new obligation has been identified that requires immediate remediation.
claims.threshold: All re-evaluation checklist items pass or have documented mitigations; next review scheduled within 90 days
claims.observed_result: 2026-Q2 quarterly review completed 2026-06-20. 14 of 14 checklist items passed. 0 items requiring immediate remediation. 2 items flagged for watch with mitigations documented. Next review scheduled 2026-09-15.

evidence_artifacts.description: Completed quarterly re-evaluation checklist for 2026-Q2, including drift analysis, incident review, regulatory change assessment, and capability regression check. Signed by the model risk officer.
Calendar entry and ownership record for next quarterly review, confirming the 2026-09-15 review date, assigned reviewer, and notification chain.

assurance_target.deployment_context: production-eu-west-1 — customer support automation, reviewed as part of enterprise model risk management program
assurance_target.use_case: Enterprise customer support automation — quarterly re-evaluation gate as required by EU AI Act Article 9 and SR 26-2 ongoing monitoring obligations

claims.decision_rationale: All 14 checklist items passed the 2026-Q2 review. Two watch items (minor latency increase trend and a pending EU AI Act Omnibus clarification on Annex III scope) were documented with mitigations and owners assigned; neither rises to the level requiring immediate remediation or model suspension. Confidence is 'medium' because this is an internal review without third-party verification. The next independent audit is scheduled for 2026-Q3. Next quarterly review is scheduled for 2026-09-15, within the 90-day re-evaluation window.
